title: Fine Tunes
description: Curate images, run a prompt, get a trained model. That's it.

content.context: Training a custom image model sounds like it should be hard — GPU provisioning, notebook wrangling, cryptic hyperparameters. It isn't, if someone breaks it down for you. Fine Tunes distills the entire Flux LoRA fine-tuning process into a single repo that an AI agent can clone, set up, and walk you through. Curate a folder of images, point the agent at it, and everything else is automated. The goal was to make model training legible — not just possible, but something a curious person could understand end to end by following along.
goals: Distill fine-tuning into succinct, digestible steps anyone can follow
Automate the full workflow so a user only needs to curate images and run a prompt
Make LoRA parameter tuning accessible through clear documentation and visual guides
Produce a showcase model that proves the pipeline works — Android Dream v4

artifacts.label: Project Identity
artifacts.description: The logo for the Python CLI that powers the entire fine-tuning workflow.

artifacts.alt: Flux style pipeline showing train, status, and generate stages
artifacts.label: Training Pipeline
artifacts.description: The full pipeline from dataset preparation through training to image generation — all orchestrated through a single CLI with three commands.

artifacts.code:
# 1. Train your model
python flux_style_finetune.py train --images-dir ./my_artwork --model-name my-style --username you

# 2. Check training status
python flux_style_finetune.py status

# 3. Generate images
python flux_style_finetune.py generate --prompt "your prompt" --model-name my-style --username you

# 3b. Transform existing images (img2img)
python flux_style_finetune.py generate --image ./photo.jpg --prompt "style it" --model-name my-style --username you
artifacts.alt: CLI usage showing the three-step workflow
artifacts.label: Three-Step Workflow
artifacts.description: The entire creative loop distilled to three commands. Train a model, check when it's ready, then generate infinite variations — including img2img style transfer.

artifacts.alt: LoRA scale guide showing style strength from subtle to dominant
artifacts.label: LoRA Scale Tuning
artifacts.description: Visual guide to the key creative control — LoRA scale determines how strongly your trained style overrides the base model, from subtle influence to full stylistic dominance.

artifacts.alt: Cost breakdown for training and generation on Replicate
artifacts.label: Cost Model
artifacts.description: Transparent cost breakdown — training a model runs about $1.50, and generation is fractions of a cent per image. Makes experimentation cheap.

artifacts.alt: Android Dream v4 hero image — painterly cyberpunk cityscape
artifacts.label: Android Dream v4
artifacts.description: The showcase model. Trained on 7 captioned images of painterly Blade Runner 2049-inspired poster art. Features atmospheric cyberpunk cityscapes with dramatic scale, bold warm-vs-cool palettes, and contemplative isolation.

outcome.caption: Android Dream v4 — atmospheric scale

outcome.alt: Generated silhouetted figure against massive structure
outcome.caption: Android Dream v4 — human scale

content.whatsNext: Next step is packaging the agent prompt so anyone can run it cold — clone the repo, provide a folder of images, and have a trained model generating in under thirty minutes. Also exploring multi-model blending and automated prompt-sweep tools for systematic style space exploration.
